name: Autonomous Vehicle CAN Bus Takeover
description: Attack tree modelling compromise of autonomous and connected vehicle systems through CAN bus injection, ADAS sensor spoofing, V2X manipulation, and telematics exploitation
root_objective: Take control of vehicle driving functions via cyber attack

nodes.title: Take Control of Autonomous Vehicle Functions
nodes.description: Compromise vehicle systems to manipulate steering, braking, acceleration, or sensor inputs

nodes.title: Inject Commands on CAN Bus
nodes.description: Access the vehicle's Controller Area Network to send forged messages to safety-critical ECUs

nodes.title: Exploit OBD-II Port Access
nodes.description: Connect to the OBD-II diagnostic port to inject CAN frames that control steering, braking, or throttle

nodes.title: Compromise Infotainment Head Unit
nodes.description: Exploit the internet-connected infotainment system and pivot to the CAN bus via gateway ECU vulnerabilities

nodes.title: Exploit Telematics Control Unit (TCU)
nodes.description: Compromise the cellular-connected TCU to gain remote access to vehicle's internal networks

nodes.title: Spoof ADAS Sensor Inputs
nodes.description: Feed false data to autonomous driving sensors to cause misperception of the environment

nodes.title: LiDAR Spoofing/Blinding
nodes.description: Use lasers to inject phantom objects into LiDAR point clouds or blind the sensor entirely

nodes.title: Camera Adversarial Patches
nodes.description: Place adversarial pattern stickers on road signs to cause misclassification by the vision system

nodes.title: GPS Spoofing for Navigation
nodes.description: Broadcast false GPS signals to redirect the vehicle's planned route

nodes.title: Attack V2X Communications
nodes.description: Exploit Vehicle-to-Everything communications to inject false safety messages

nodes.title: Inject False V2I Messages
nodes.description: Broadcast fabricated Vehicle-to-Infrastructure messages (e.g., false traffic light states, speed limits)

nodes.title: Spoof V2V Emergency Braking
nodes.description: Send spoofed V2V messages that trigger automatic emergency braking in surrounding vehicles

nodes.title: Attack OTA Update Mechanism
nodes.description: Compromise the over-the-air firmware update system to deploy malicious ECU firmware

nodes.title: Man-in-the-Middle OTA Update
nodes.description: Intercept and modify OTA firmware packages during download to inject malicious ECU code
